name: sequencelib

metadata.name: sequencelib
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: Read, write, and analyze biological sequences

documentation:
# sequencelib

![](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-2.23.0-blue)
[![PyPI downloads](https://static.pepy.tech/personalized-badge/sequencelib?period=total&units=none&left_color=black&right_color=blue&left_text=PyPI%20downloads&service=github)](https://pepy.tech/project/sequencelib)

Using classes and methods in sequencelib.py it is possible to read and write textfiles containing DNA or protein-sequences (possibly aligned) and to analyze and manipulate the sequence in various ways.

## Availability

The sequencelib.py module is available on GitHub: https://github.com/agormp/sequencelib and on PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/sequencelib/

## Installation

```
python3 -m pip install sequencelib
```

Upgrading to latest version:

```
python3 -m pip install --upgrade sequencelib
```


## Highlights

To be written

modules.name: sequencelib
documentation: Classes and methods for reading, analyzing, manipulating, and writing DNA and protein sequences
By Anders Gorm Pedersen, agpe@dtu.dk, Technical University of Denmark, Bioinformatics, 2012-2024

functions.name: sequencelib.find_seqtype
documentation: Various functions used by methods, that do not fit neatly in any class

functions.name: sequencelib.seqtype_attributes
documentation: Returns alphabet and ambigsymbols for given seqtype

functions.name: sequencelib._indices
documentation: Helper function that finds indices of substring in string. Returns as set

functions.name: sequencelib._multi_indices
documentation:
Helper function: finds indices of all occurences of substrings in mystring
substrings: iterable with multiple strings.
Result is returned as set of indices

functions.name: sequencelib.remove_comments
documentation:
Takes input string and strips away commented text, delimited by 'leftdelim' and 'rightdelim'.
Also deals with nested comments.

functions.name: sequencelib.make_sparseencoder
documentation: Returns function that can sparse-encode strings in specified alphabet

functions.name: sequencelib.main
documentation: Execute Test code (run module in standalone mode)

classes.name: sequencelib.SeqError
documentation: Seqlib exceptions

classes.name: sequencelib.Const
documentation: Global constants used by seqlib

classes.name: sequencelib.Sequence

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.__eq__
documentation: Implements equality check between sequences

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.__ne__
documentation: Implements inequality checking between sequences

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.__len__
documentation: Implements the len() operator for sequences

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.__getitem__
documentation: Implements indexing, slicing, and iteration for sequences

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.__setitem__
documentation: Allows change of single residues by using dict-like syntax: seq[5] = 'A' for instance

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.__str__
documentation: String (fasta) representation of sequence

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.copy_seqobject
documentation: Customized deep copy of sequence object

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.rename
documentation: Changes name of sequence

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.subseq
documentation: Returns subsequence as sequence object of proper type. Indexing can start at zero or one

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.subseqpos
documentation:
Returns subsequence containing residues on selected positions as
sequence object of proper type. Indexing can start at zero or one

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.appendseq
documentation:
Appends seq from other to end of self. Returns as new Sequence object.
Name from self is retained

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.prependseq
documentation:
Prepends seq from other before start of self. Returns as new Sequence object.
Name from self is retained

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.windows
documentation: Returns window iterator object

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.remgaps
documentation: Removes gap characters from sequence

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.shuffle
documentation: Returns shuffled sequence as object of proper subclass

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.indexfilter
documentation: Discards symbols at positions that are not in keeplist (seq changed in place)

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.seqdiff
documentation:
Returns list of tuples summarizing difference between seqs in self and other:
    (site, residue1, residue2)
Option zeroindex=False causes site numbering to start at 1 (otherwise 0)

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.hamming
documentation: Directly observable distance between self and other (absolute count of different positions)

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.hamming_ignoregaps
documentation: Like hamming distance, but positions with gaps are ignored

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.pdist
documentation: Directly observable distance between self and other (in differences per site)

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.pdist_ignoregaps
documentation: Like pdist distance, but positions with gaps are ignored

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.pdist_ignorechars
documentation: Like pdist_ignoregaps, but ignoring positions with ANY of the characters in igchars

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.residuecounts
documentation: Returns dictionary with counts of residues for single seq. {letter:count}

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.composition
documentation: Returns dictionary with composition for single seq. {letter:(count,freq)}

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.findgaps
documentation: Returns list of tuples giving (start,stop) indices for any gaps in sequence

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.fasta
documentation: Return fasta-formatted sequence as a string

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.how
documentation: Return how-formatted sequence as a string

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.gapencoded
documentation: Returns gap-encoding of sequence (gap=1, nongap=0) as a string

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.tab
documentation:
Returns tab-formatted sequence as a string:
name TAB seq TAB annotation TAB comments
If no annotation is present, comments are placed after two TABs if present

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequence.raw
documentation: Returns raw-formatted sequence as a string
Baseclass for sequence classes

methods.name: sequencelib.DNA_sequence.revcomp
documentation: Returns reverse complement sequence as DNA sequence object - original is unchanged

methods.name: sequencelib.DNA_sequence.translate
documentation:
Translates DNA sequence to protein. Returns Protein_sequence object.
reading_frame: 1, 2, or 3, where 1 means start translation at first
nucleotide in sequence. Translation includes as many full-length
codons as possible.
Class containing one DNA sequence, and corresponding methods

classes.name: sequencelib.Protein_sequence

methods.name: sequencelib.Protein_sequence.shuffle
documentation: Returns shuffled sequence as protein sequence object - original is unchanged
Class containing one protein sequence, and corresponding methods

classes.name: sequencelib.ASCII_sequence

methods.name: sequencelib.ASCII_sequence.__init__
documentation: Python note: will this ever be used???

methods.name: sequencelib.ASCII_sequence.shuffle
documentation: Returns shuffled sequence as ASCII sequence object - original is unchanged
Class containing one sequence containing ASCII letters, and corresponding methods

classes.name: sequencelib.Restriction_sequence
documentation: Sequence containing restriction data-type (presence/absence - e.g. gaps)

classes.name: sequencelib.Standard_sequence
documentation: Sequence containing standard data-type (e.g., morphological)

classes.name: sequencelib.Mixed_sequence

methods.name: sequencelib.Mixed_sequence.__init__
documentation: Python note: I am not keeping track of original sequence types in Sequence objects
This information is available only in Seq_alignment objects. Rethink approach?
It means I no longer have access to type-specific methods such as translation
Python note: perhaps better to rethink seqtypes and to have collection of seqs in seq?
Sequence containing mix of different sequence types (e.g., restriction and DNA)

classes.name: sequencelib.Contig

methods.name: sequencelib.Contig.__init__
documentation: Initialises Contig object with one Sequence object

methods.name: sequencelib.Contig.findoverlap
documentation:
Checks if two Contig objects overlap (one contained in other, or 3' of one = 5' of other).
Minoverlap: overlap has to be at least this large to call a match

methods.name: sequencelib.Contig.merge
documentation:
Merges Contig object with other Contig given overlap info from findoverlap.
Keeps track of positions of all reads from both Contigs

methods.name: sequencelib.Contig.regions
documentation:
Divides contig into regions based on read overlaps:
        region boundaries are created in all locations where a read starts or stops.
Returns list of regions sorted by start pos. Information for a single region is stored in a special class and includes:
    start, stop pos of region (in contig coordinates)
    list of reads in region
        for each read: name, and start + stop pos (in read-coordinates)

class_variables.name: sequencelib.Contig.count
documentation: Used to keep track of number of instances of class, for individual naming of contigs

Class corresponding to contig, i.e. assembly of several reads (sequences).
Has methods for checking Contig overlap, assembly, etc.

classes.name: sequencelib.Read_assembler

methods.name: sequencelib.Read_assembler.__init__
documentation:
Initialises set of reads from Seq_set (or other iterable containing Sequence objects).
If Seq_set is not provided then individual seqs can be added later

methods.name: sequencelib.Read_assembler.addseq
documentation: For adding one sequence at a time to Read_assembler

methods.name: sequencelib.Read_assembler.assemble
documentation:
Assembles reads into as few contigs as possible, and returns list of Contig objects.
minoverlap: minimum length of overlap required to merge reads.
Special value minoverlap = -1: set value automatically based on size of data set
Class for assembling reads into contigs

classes.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.__init__
documentation: Implementation note: This baseclass contains methods and attributes that are
common to all sequence collections. Subclasses then implement methods and attributes
that are specific to aligned or un-aligned sequence collections.
This class should NOT be instantiated.

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.__len__
documentation: Implements the len() operator for sequence sets (=number of seqs)

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.__getitem__
documentation:
Implements indexing, slicing, and iteration for sequences in seqlist.
Integer index returns Sequence object
Slice returns subset (Seq_set or Seq_alignment object depending on what subclass we are in).
Tuple ([row, column] indexing) returns subsequence of selected sequence(s)

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.__setitem__
documentation: Implements setting Sequence objects in seqlist using integer indices.

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.__eq__
documentation: Implements equality check between sequence collections

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.__ne__
documentation: Implements inequality check between sequence collections

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.__str__
documentation: String (fasta) representation of sequence collection

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.sortnames
documentation: Sorts sequences in object alphabetically by name

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.addseq
documentation: Add Sequence object to set

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.addseqset
documentation: Adds all sequences in 'other' to collection in 'self'

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.remseq
documentation: Remove Sequence object from set

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.remseqs
documentation: Remove named sequences from set

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.changeseqname
documentation: Change the name of one sequence object from oldname to newname

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.getseq
documentation: Returns Sequence object for named sequence

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.subset
documentation: Returns new sequence collection object (of proper type) containing named sequences only

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.subsample
documentation: Returns new sequence collection object (of proper type) containing samplesize randomly selected sequences

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.subseq
documentation: Returns specified positions as Seq_alignment or Seq_set object (depending on class of self)

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.getnames
documentation: Returns list of names of all sequences in set

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.range
documentation: In-place selection of subsequences covering positions rangefrom:rangeto

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.group_identical_seqs
documentation: Return list of lists of names for each unique sequence: [[s1], [s2,s3], [s4]]

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.residuecounts
documentation: Returns dictionary with cumulated counts for set of seqs {symbol:count}

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.composition
documentation: Returns dictionary with cumulated counts AND freq for set of seqs {symbol:[count,freq]}

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.clean_names
documentation:
Change all sequence names such that chars in 'illegal' are replaced by 'rep'.
For instance before creating phylogenetic tree from sequences

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.rename_numbered
documentation: Renames all sequences in collection to this form: basename_001, ...

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.rename_regexp
documentation: Renames all sequences in collection: replace occurrences of old_regex with new_string

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.transname
documentation: Translate all names using oldname/newname pairs in namefile. E.g., use to restore names after renaming

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.revcomp
documentation: If DNA: Returns new sequence collection (of same type as original) reverse complemented

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.translate
documentation:
If seqtype is DNA: Returns new sequence collection with seqtype protein
reading_frame: 1, 2, or 3, where 1 means start translation at first
nucleotide in sequences. Translation includes as many full-length
codons as possible.

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.fasta
documentation: Returns fasta-formatted set of sequences as a string

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.how
documentation: Returns HOW-formatted set of sequences as a string

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.tab
documentation: Returns tab-formatted set of sequences as a string

methods.name: sequencelib.Sequences_base.raw
documentation: Returns raw-formatted set of sequences as a string
Baseclass for classes that contain a number of sequences - don't instantiate!

classes.name: sequencelib.Seq_set

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_set.__init__
documentation: Note: most functionality is in abstract baseclass "Sequences_base"
This derived class only contains methods that should never be used on aligned sequences

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_set.remgaps
documentation: Removes gap characters from all sequences in Seq_set
Class containing a set of sequences

classes.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.copy_alignobject
documentation:
Returns copy of Seq_alignment object. Copies standard attributes.
Any user-added attributes are not copied. Similar to effect of copy.deepcopy

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.addseq
documentation: Add Sequence object to alignment

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.appendalignment
documentation: Appends sequences in 'other' to the end of similarly named sequences in 'self'

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.alignlen
documentation: Returns length of alignment or zero if no sequences

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.getcolumn
documentation: Returns column 'i' as list

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.columns
documentation: Returns column iterator object

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.samplecols
documentation: Randomly selects samplesize columns from alignment, discarding rest

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.conscols
documentation: Returns list of columns that are conserved

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.varcols
documentation: Returns list of columns that are variable (not conserved)

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.gappycols
documentation: Returns list of columns that are gappy (contain one or more gaps)

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.site_summary
documentation: Returns tuple with summary of sites: (alignlen, n_var_cols, n_gappy_cols)

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.indexfilter
documentation:
Discards all columns whose indices are not in keeplist.
Note: works in-place, nothing is returned

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.remcols
documentation: Discards all columns whose indices are in discardlist

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.remambigcol
documentation: Removes all columns that contain one or more ambiguity symbols

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.remfracambigcol
documentation: Removes all columns where >= "frac" fraction of residues are ambiguity symbols

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.remgapcol
documentation: Removes all columns that contain one or more gaps

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.remfracgapcol
documentation: Removes all columns where >= "frac" fraction of residues are gaps

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.remendgapcol
documentation:
Removes columns from both ends of alignment where > frac fraction of sequences
have endgaps (contiguous gappy region starting at either end of alignment)

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.endgapfraclist
documentation:
Returns list giving, for each column, the fraction of sequences having endgaps.
Endgaps: contiguous gappy region starting at either end of alignment

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.remendgapseqs
documentation: Remove sequences with endgaps >= cutoff

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.remconscol
documentation: Removes columns where all symbols are identical (conserved columns)

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.rem_hmmalign_insertcol
documentation: For alignments created by HMMer:hmmalign: Removes insert state columns

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.findgaps
documentation: Returns list of (start, stop) index-tuples for gaps found across sequences

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.gap_encode
documentation:
Return Seq_alignment object with binary (Restriction-type) seqs
encoding all gaps in alignment. Each unique, possibly multi-residue, gap is 
encoded as 1 if present and 0 if not. Order of columns based on sorted list of
start, stop positions (so (2, 5) is before (2, 6) for instance)

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.align_seq_pos_cache_builder
documentation: Helper function for seqpos2alignpos and alignpos2seqpos: Builds mapping caches for given seq

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.seqpos2alignpos
documentation:
Input: seqname and position in sequence. Output: alignment position corresponding to seqpos.
Slicesyntax = True: All numbering starts at 0. Slicesyntax = False: All numbering starts at 1

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.alignpos2seqpos
documentation:
Input: seqname and position in alignment. Output: tuple of (seqpos, GAPSTATUS)
If alignpos corresponds to a gap, GAPSTATUS will be True, and seqpos is pos of preceding residue in seq.
Slicesyntax = True: Numbering starts at 0. Slicesyntax = False: Numbering starts at 1

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.shannon
documentation: Computes Shannon entropy for all columns in the alignment, returns list of values (index=position in seq)

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.nucfreq
documentation:
Computes nucleotide frequency distribution for requested columns in the alignment (default: all).
Returns matrix of values: Rows = A, C, G, T. Column=position in seq.

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.consensus
documentation:
Returns consensus sequence (= most frequent symbol in each alignment column) as sequence object.
Ties are broken randomly

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.seqset
documentation: Removes gaps from Seq_alignment object and returns as Seq_set object

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.partitions_as_seqalignments
documentation: Returns list containing all partitions as Seq_alignment objects

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.distdict
documentation: Returns nested dict of pairwise sequence distances: distdict[name1][name2] = dist

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.sequence_diversity
documentation:
Compute pairwise sequence diversity (known as pi for nucleotide sequences).
Return mean, and standard deviation, as tuple: (mean, std)
Discard pairwise gappy and/or ambiguous positions if requested.

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.pairwise_sequence_distances
documentation:
Compute all pairwise sequence distances.
Return a DataFrame with columns: 'seq1', 'seq2', 'distance'

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.overlap
documentation: Computes overlap between two alignments as fraction of identically paired residues

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.phylip
documentation: Returns phylip-formatted, interleaved alignment as string

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.clustal
documentation: Returns clustal-formatted alignment as string

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.nexus
documentation: Returns nexus-formatted alignment as string

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.nexusgap
documentation: Returns nexus-formatted alignment with binary gap encoding, mixed mrbayes format, as string

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.charsetblock
documentation: Returns MrBayes block with charset commands for partitioned analysis as string

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.mbpartblock
documentation: Returns MrBayes block for partitioned analysis as string

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.bestblock
documentation: Returns MrBayes block for BEST (species tree) analysis as string

methods.name: sequencelib.Seq_alignment.nexuspart
documentation: Returns MrBayes block for partitioned data set as string
Class representing aligned set of sequences

classes.name: sequencelib.Seqfile_reader

methods.name: sequencelib.Seqfile_reader.makeseq
documentation:
Takes name, sequence, annotation, and comments, returns sequence object of correct type.
Called by subclass

methods.name: sequencelib.Seqfile_reader.readseq
documentation: Reads single sequence from file, returns as sequence object

methods.name: sequencelib.Seqfile_reader.read_seqs
documentation: Reads all sequences, returns them as Seq_set object

methods.name: sequencelib.Seqfile_reader.read_alignment

classes.name: sequencelib.Fastafilehandle

methods.name: sequencelib.Fastafilehandle.__next__
documentation: Parser function, returns next seq as Sequence object
Reader class for fasta files

classes.name: sequencelib.Howfilehandle

methods.name: sequencelib.Howfilehandle.__next__
documentation: Parser function, returns next seq as Sequence object
Reader class for HOW files

classes.name: sequencelib.Genbankfilehandle

methods.name: sequencelib.Genbankfilehandle.__next__
documentation: Parser function, returns next seq as Sequence object
Reader class for GenBank files

classes.name: sequencelib.Tabfilehandle

methods.name: sequencelib.Tabfilehandle.__next__
documentation: Parser function, returns next seq as Sequence object
Reader class for TAB-files

classes.name: sequencelib.Rawfilehandle

methods.name: sequencelib.Rawfilehandle.__next__
documentation: Parser function, returns next seq as Sequence object
Reader class for RAW-files

classes.name: sequencelib.Alignfile_reader

methods.name: sequencelib.Alignfile_reader.__init__
documentation: Note: Unlike non-alignment sequence files, alignment files do not have iteration defined:
You typically will have to read the entire alignment before processing

methods.name: sequencelib.Alignfile_reader.makeseq
documentation: Takes name, sequence, annotation, and comments, returns sequence object of correct type
Baseclass for alignment-type sequence file readers

classes.name: sequencelib.Clustalfilehandle

methods.name: sequencelib.Clustalfilehandle.read_alignment
documentation: Reads all sequences, returns them as Seq_alignment object
Reader class for Clustal alignment files

classes.name: sequencelib.Phylipfilehandle

methods.name: sequencelib.Phylipfilehandle.read_alignment
documentation: Reads all sequences, returns them as Seq_alignment object

Reader class for Phylip alignment files.
Only parses interleaved format (or sequential where seq is on same line as name)

classes.name: sequencelib.Nexusfilehandle

methods.name: sequencelib.Nexusfilehandle.read_alignment
documentation: Reads all sequences, returns them as Seq_alignment object
Reader class for NEXUS alignment files

classes.name: sequencelib.Stockholmfilehandle

methods.name: sequencelib.Stockholmfilehandle.__init__
documentation: NOTE: currently ignores metadata

methods.name: sequencelib.Stockholmfilehandle.read_alignment
documentation: Reads all sequences, returns them as Seq_alignment object
Reader class for STOCKHOLM format alignment files

classes.name: sequencelib.Seqfile

methods.name: sequencelib.Seqfile.__new__
documentation: This is handy for avoiding long "elif" statements when selecting among several filehandles
Also implements filetype autodetection (should that go somewhere else?)
Factory for constructing seqfilehandle objects. Type selected based on fileformat string

methods.name: sequencelib.vcf.__init__
documentation: NOTE: rewrite to base parsing on actual info in INFO header.
Or perhaps simply to take into account most common fields in INFO:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Variant_Call_Format

methods.name: sequencelib.vcf.from_vcffile
documentation: Constructor 1: constructs vcf object from standard VCF file
VCF file format (header lines + example of SNP)
##INFO=<ID=AF,Number=1,Type=Float,Description="allele frequency for ALT allele">
##INFO=<ID=DP,Number=1,Type=Integer,Description="Raw read depth">
##INFO=<ID=DP4,Number=4,Type=Integer,Description="# high-quality (i.e. after filtering) ref-forward bases, ref-reverse, alt-forward and alt-reverse bases">
##INFO=<ID=SB,Number=1,Type=Integer,Description="Phred-scaled strand bias at this position">
##INFO=<ID=CONSVAR,Number=0,Type=Flag,Description="Indicates that the variant is a consensus variant (as opposed to a low frequency variant).">
#CHROM                   POS    ID  REF  ALT  QUAL  FILTER     INFO

methods.name: sequencelib.vcf.from_vcfcountfile
documentation: Constructor 2: constructs vcf object from VCF counts file
Homegrown, pre-parsed format looking as follows:
#POS  A   C   G   T
